author: @consciousenergies
text:
I had something come across my radar that I would like to attempt but you have a better understanding of this particular chemistry.  Let me know what you think and if its viable. I am all about open sourcing so if you feel like this is interesting and you want to pursue it, feel free.  Its the idea of growing graphene films using cellulose.  Have you ever heard of Kombucha?  It might be possible to make a graphene sheet of sorts by growing whats referred to as a SCOBY. There is much talk in the green chemistry community about growing your own cloths using this process, so I thought perhaps there is a way to grow graphene in this nano cellulose symbiotic bacteria/yeast?  Its just a thought, but it would be easy to produce and quite green. Plus its great for the belly ;)  There is a ton of videos on how to create this culture but here is one for you so you don't have to bother searching.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uTs6bJ-O-1Q
Not sure if there is any fruit to bare, perhaps once I can attain some GO I will give it a go ;)   Much love to you and yours brother form across the pond.﻿

replies.author: @MongrelShark
replies.text: I read "reeferd to as a SCOBY" fist time. lol. I wonder what my subconscious is trying to tell me?

replies.author: @cavemanelectronics9611
replies.text: a buddy is supposed to bring me some of his latest kombucha. strawberry flavor! after seeing it in a jar for so long im not sure im ready to drink it yet...im afraid!

replies.author: @consciousenergies
replies.text: It's a pretty amazing probiotic elixir. Its taste is usually like a light apple cider vinegar. I have never had strawberry though, that's sounds interesting. :)

replies.author: @consciousenergies
replies.text: Those damn spelling errors! Thanks Mongrel Shark lol
